mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 retail sales at 549256M suggests sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to precede positive SPX returns in > 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor markets and accelerating wage growth, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate labor costs. This dual effect produces negative equity returns as margin compression dominates over the 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 UNRATE at 6.9% suggests room for further tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE falls below 5.0%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 60-day returns exceeds +0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to tighter financial conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% (lagged) suggests recent tightening cycle context
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day returns is less negative than -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when supply-side capacity utilization rises, signaling robust manufacturing activity and improving corporate profitability. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 INDPRO at 101.5055 indicates near-peak industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO momentum fails to precede positive returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the interaction effect amplifies confidence in sustained economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 HOUST at 1415K and RSAFS at 549256M both elevated
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS grow, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals wage-price spiral dynamics. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the Federal Reserve responds with further tightening, compressing equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 200K/month and CPIAUCSL inflation > 3.5% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 PAYEMS at 142373K and CPIAUCSL at 260.325 suggest tight labor and goods markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) above 2.0% signal restrictive monetary conditions that suppress equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days only when the Fed is actively tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 real rates elevated in tightening cycle context
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with positive real rates
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances when real rates exceed 2.0%, or Rank IC between real rate spread and 20-day returns is less negative than -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2.5% annualized signals weakening economic momentum and reduced earnings growth expectations. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2006 GDPC1 at 18583.984B reflects Q1 2006 growth trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns exceeds -0.02.
